title: `quiver:squash-spec` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver squash-spec <spec>` that operates on already-archived specs (v21) to optionally collapse their git history into a single historical commit, or just annotate the archive with a compressed HISTORY_COLLAPSED.md note.
description: Once the archive grows, `git log` on the active tree still shows every merge commit from archived specs. This command offers two modes: a safe annotation-only mode (default) that adds a summary file, and an optional `--rewrite-history` mode that rebases the archive commits into one. History rewriting is destructive and requires explicit opt-in and a clean working tree.
must: Require the target spec to be under `specs/_archive/<spec>/`
Default mode: write `specs/_archive/<spec>/HISTORY_COLLAPSED.md` with summary of commits (author, date, subject)
`--rewrite-history`: perform an interactive-style rebase programmatically; block if the working tree is dirty, require `--yes` confirmation
Abort history rewrite with a clear message if the target spec's commits touched files outside `specs/<spec>/` (unsafe)
not_included: Remote history rewriting (users run `git push --force` themselves if they want)
Auto-selecting which commits to squash (command operates on the spec as a whole)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver squash-spec <spec>` on an unarchived spec exits non-zero
Default mode writes HISTORY_COLLAPSED.md without touching git
`--rewrite-history --yes` with clean tree rewrites commits for that spec into one
`--rewrite-history` on a dirty tree exits non-zero with a clear message
`--rewrite-history` refuses when the spec's commits touched files outside the spec directory
assumptions: Default annotation-only mode is enough for most users; history rewriting is rare
Users understand that `--rewrite-history` is destructive and forces a re-push
Commits that touch `specs/<spec>/` exclusively are safe to squash; mixed commits are not
blocked_reason: Deferred: needs ≥20 archived specs and documented friction
